originalTitle: New York governor says she’s using AI to analyze ‘every single rule’ in the state
summary: New York Governor Kathy Hochul might have just signed a moratorium on new AI data centers in the state, but she's not against using the technology herself. During an interview with Bloomberg's Odd Lots podcast, Hochul said that her team is using "AI to analyze every single rule, regulation, [and] policy" to check for outdated […]
content: New York Governor Kathy Hochul might have just signed a moratorium on new AI data centers in the state, but she’s not against using the technology herself. During an interview with Bloomberg’s Odd Lots podcast, Hochul said that her team is using “AI to analyze every single rule, regulation, [and] policy” to check for outdated legislation. Some of the antiquated laws mentioned by Hochul in the interview include a $25 fee required to take a dog hunting, or a stipulation that pregnant people need a permit to work after midnight. Hochul added that it “probably would have taken five years at the staff level” to review all of the laws in the state, but with AI, her team “did it in a couple of months.” This will allow Hochul and state agencies “to get rid of” these outdated regulations, she said.  [Media: https://youtu.be/XJCFzIjr7EA?si=8M0rDOKWwLSYdCTE&t=577] Earlier this week, New York became the first state to put a pause on new hyperscale data centers for up to a year. In the meantime, state lawmakers plan to come up with regulations to protect residents from the rising utility costs and the threat to natural resources posed by massive data centers. “I want a government that’s not on your back but on your side, and using AI has been powerful to do that,” Hochul told Bloomberg. “I think every level of government should use this… I’m going to make dramatic changes using the power of AI.”
chineseTitle: 纽约州州长表示，她正在使用 AI 来分析该州的“每一条规则”
chineseSummary: 纽约州州长 Kathy Hochul 可能刚刚签署了暂停在该州新建人工智能数据中心的协议，但她并不反对自己使用该技术。在接受彭博社 Odd Lot 播客采访时，Hochul 表示，她的团队正在使用“人工智能来分析每一项规则、法规和政策”，以检查是否有过时的 [...]
chineseContent: 纽约州州长 Kathy Hochul 可能刚刚签署了暂停在该州新建人工智能数据中心的协议，但她并不反对自己使用该技术。在接受彭博社 Odd Lot 播客采访时，Hochul 表示，她的团队正在使用“人工智能来分析每一项规则、法规和政策”，以检查是否有过时的立法。 Hochul 在采访中提到的一些过时的法律包括猎狗需要缴纳 25 美元的费用，或者孕妇需要获得许可证才能在午夜后工作的规定。 Hochul 补充说，“在工作人员层面上可能需要五年时间”来审查该州的所有法律，但有了人工智能，她的团队“在几个月内就完成了”。她说，这将使 Hochul 和国家机构能够“废除”这些过时的法规。  [媒体：https://youtu.be/XJCFzIjr7EA?si=8M0rDOKWwLSYdCTE&t=577] 本周早些时候，纽约成为第一个暂停新建超大规模数据中心长达一年的州。与此同时，州立法者计划制定法规，保护居民免受公用事业成本上升以及大型数据中心对自然资源构成的威胁。 Hochul 告诉彭博社：“我希望政府不是支持你，而是站在你一边，而使用人工智能可以有效地做到这一点。” “我认为各级政府都应该利用这个……我将利用人工智能的力量做出巨大的改变。”
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
